title: Pleurothallis serrulata
taxonomy: Pleurothallis serrulata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This classification makes it a member of the largest family of flowering plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Pleurothallis serrulata belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Pleurothallis is a group of terrestrial orchids, and within this genus, P. serrulata is one of the many species found. The specific classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics of this plant species.

intro: Pleurothallis serrulata, commonly known as the serrated pleurothallis or toothed pleurothallis, is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothallis serrulata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pleurothallis serrulata is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tinctures, to take advantage of its health benefits.
